task.title: List persisted tasks associated with a session
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/482 by adding a deterministic read-only membership query over the existing immutable association from task to session without introducing a duplicate projection table, reassociation, pagination, runtime, identity, provider, or permission policy.
acceptance_criteria: An existing session returns all associated active and terminal tasks in ascending task identifier order with each task replayed to its latest state.
Unassociated tasks and tasks belonging to other sessions are excluded, while closed and reopened sessions retain membership across database reopen.
A missing session returns the existing typed domain error and listing appends no events.
Malformed payloads and associations whose payload task does not match the owning stream remain explicit replay or invalid-history errors.
Documentation, independent review, the local repository gate, CodeRabbit, and the hosted Rust quality gate pass before squash merge.

attempts.summary: Created bounded issue 482 and wrote session membership acceptance tests before production support.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused RED suite failed to compile because TaskStore had no list_for_session method.
attempts.patch: Added integration scenarios for deterministic filtering and ordering, latest terminal state, database reopen, closed and reopened sessions, missing sessions, no-write behavior, malformed payloads, and stream-to-payload mismatch.

attempts.summary: Implemented filtered typed event replay and the task membership query.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused suite passed after the query verified the session, decoded persisted task.session_associated events, replayed each matching task history, validated stream ownership, and sorted the resulting tasks by opaque identifier bytes.
attempts.patch: Added a crate-private replay_event_type helper and public TaskStore::list_for_session without adding a mutable session-side index or new lifecycle event.

attempts.summary: Made read-only and corruption behavior observable in tests and documentation.
attempts.diagnostic: Event counts remained unchanged across listing calls, valid terminal state was returned from full task replay, and corrupted association payloads surfaced as Replay or InvalidHistory rather than being skipped.
attempts.patch: Extended association integration coverage and updated the task lifecycle, session lifecycle, README milestone, and explicit non-goals.

attempts.summary: Performed independent pre-commit review and full local verification.
attempts.diagnostic: An independent Codex review identified no blocking or follow-up defect; pinned just verify passed formatting, checking, 109 workspace tests, Clippy with warnings denied, SecretSpec integration, and diff checks.
attempts.patch: Retained the replay design owned by the task aggregate after review and committed the implementation, tests, and documentation as one bounded Conventional Commit.

attempts.summary: Published, reviewed, and squash-merged the query capability.
attempts.diagnostic: GitHub Rust quality gate run 29459097365 passed, CodeRabbit reported no actionable comments and all five pre-merge checks passed, and pull request 483 squash-merged as 38a7053944190be8891620e2fd0abf43784e19af.
attempts.patch: Linked issue 482, enabled squash auto-merge, monitored both required checks, and synchronized local main after merge.

attempts.summary: Captured and validated the merged episode in the Vela-native corpus.
attempts.diagnostic: The schema-v1 record validator accepted the artifact, complete corpus inspection reported 39 valid and zero invalid records, and pinned full verification passed with the record included.
attempts.patch: Added one positive curated record with RED and GREEN attempts, replay rationale, corruption diagnostics, review evidence, lessons, and public merged provenance.

outcome.summary: Vela can now recover the latest persisted tasks belonging to any existing session in deterministic order while preserving association history owned by each task and surfacing corrupt data explicitly.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test task_session_association # RED: compile failure because list_for_session did not exist
nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test task_session_association # GREEN: 13 passed, 0 failed
nix develop --command just verify # formatting, checking, 109 workspace tests, Clippy, SecretSpec, and diff gates passed
nix develop --command codex review --uncommitted # no blocking or follow-up findings
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29459097365 and required CodeRabbit status for pull request 483 # passed
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0039-session-membership.json # valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # 39 valid, 0 invalid
nix develop --command just verify # all repository gates passed with the development record included

lessons: A reverse membership view does not require a second mutable aggregate when the authoritative association event owned by the task already carries both identifiers.
A read model should replay each matching aggregate to its latest state rather than reconstructing partial task state from the association row.
Deterministic ordering belongs in the query contract because SQLite row order and append chronology are not stable membership semantics.
Read-only behavior deserves an event-count assertion even when the implementation appears structurally incapable of writing.
A reverse query must validate that the association payload task identifier matches the stream that owns the event; otherwise corrupt cross-stream data can impersonate another task.
Closed and reopened session status should not erase membership because the immutable relation remains owned by task history.
A linear scan is the smallest correct bootstrap design; pagination or a derived index should wait for measured scale and explicit consistency requirements.
